sn.s: Ha pasado más de un año desde que llegué.
sn.e: More than a year has passed since I arrived.

sn.s: Una tarde, miro a mi alrededor y reflexiono.
sn.e: One afternoon, I look around and reflect.

sn.s: Tengo a Lucía, su familia, mis amigos.
sn.e: I have Lucía, her family, my friends.

sn.s: Llegué sin saber una palabra de español.
sn.e: I arrived without knowing a word of Spanish.

sn.s: Ahora hablo, río, y amo en este idioma.
sn.e: Now I speak, laugh, and love in this language.
"What are you thinking about?" Lucía asks me.

sn.s: "En lo afortunado que soy," le respondo.
sn.e: "About how lucky I am," I answer.
"Here I found roots I didn't expect."

sn.s: "Te valoro a vos y a todo lo que tengo."
sn.e: "I value you and everything I have."

sn.s: Lo que importa no son las cosas, sino la gente.
sn.e: What matters isn't things, but people.

sn.s: "Ojalá nunca olvide de dónde vengo," pienso.
sn.e: "I hope I never forget where I come from," I think.

sn.s: Aprender un idioma fue, al final, aprender a pertenecer.
sn.e: Learning a language was, in the end, learning to belong.
